title: Ultrasonic Sensor
subtitle: measure distance with sound!
overview: This session introduces students to the Ultrasonic Sensor and how it measures distance using sound waves. Students will learn about echo, distance calculation, Arduino connections, and real-world applications like obstacle detection, automatic doors, robots, and parking systems.

images._note: Add actual photos from the session — ultrasonic sensor close-up, Arduino wiring, obstacle detection activity, student projects.

activity.title: Distance Measurement using Ultrasonic Sensor
steps: Connect VCC pin to 5V on Arduino
Connect TRIG pin to Arduino digital pin 9
Connect ECHO pin to Arduino digital pin 10
Place an object in front of the sensor
Observe distance values changing in centimeters

quiz.q: What does an Ultrasonic Sensor measure?
quiz.explanation: Ultrasonic sensors are mainly used to measure distance using sound waves.

quiz.q: Which type of waves are used in an Ultrasonic Sensor?
quiz.explanation: Ultrasonic sensors use high-frequency sound waves to detect objects.

quiz.q: What is the function of the TRIG pin?
quiz.explanation: The TRIG pin sends ultrasonic pulses toward the object.

quiz.q: What is the function of the ECHO pin?
quiz.explanation: The ECHO pin receives the reflected ultrasonic signal.
The sensor measures the time taken for sound to return back as echo.

quiz.q: Which Arduino board is commonly used with HC-SR04?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO is commonly used for beginner sensor projects.

quiz.q: What happens when an object comes closer to the sensor?
quiz.explanation: As the object moves closer, the measured distance becomes smaller.

quiz.q: Which real-life device uses ultrasonic sensors?
quiz.explanation: Cars use ultrasonic sensors for reverse parking assistance.

quiz.q: What is the approximate unit used to display measured distance?
quiz.explanation: Distance is commonly displayed in centimeters (cm).

quiz.q: Why is the Ultrasonic Sensor useful in robots?
quiz.explanation: Robots use ultrasonic sensors to avoid hitting obstacles.
HC-SR04 is the model name of a popular ultrasonic sensor module.

quiz.q: What is needed to program the Ultrasonic Sensor project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to upload code to Arduino boards.
